content.full_text:
April 30, 1990

Wayne Imberri
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Wayne:

This will confirm our telephone conversation on April 26, 1990. I provided you with the following facts.

We represent a corporation that is the employer of a lobbyist. The corporation files a quarterly Form 635. The lobbyist is an outside contract lobbyist having his own firm. The corporation has recently acquired another corporation as a subsidiary. The subsidiary has a different name and also is an employer of a different lobbyist. It has been filing its own Form 635 under its own name. Its lobbyist is also an outside contract lobbyist having his own firm.

I asked if the two corporations would be required to consolidate their Form 635 reporting, or possibly cross reference each other's report, under circumstances where a representative of the parent corporation would control or direct the lobbying activities and related payments of both entities. I clarified that each entity is making its own payments from its own corporate treasuries.

After conferring with Carla Wardlow, you advised me that no consolidation or cross-referencing is required unless the parent corporation were to make the payments on behalf of the subsidiary corporation. Under such circumstances, all of the registration and authorization forms would remain unchanged.

Thank you for your assistance. Please advise me if anything herein has been misstated.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Peter A. Bagatelas
